source: Generating Alpha — Ep.56: career arc, semis, NVDA/Tesla, AI infra cycles
summary: Nvidia is one of the most exceptional companies Baker has followed for 25 years; Jensen Huang is among the two or three most exceptional people he has ever met
summary_extended: Baker argues that Nvidia represents one of the most exceptional companies he has encountered in 25 years of investing, with Jensen Huang standing alongside Hock Tan and Avago/Broadcom and Lisa Su of AMD as among the two or three most exceptional people he has ever met in the semiconductor industry. His conviction is grounded in continuous, deep coverage of Nvidia since its small-cap days in 2000, having read every earnings transcript and maintained a personal relationship with Jensen from his early career as a sell-side analyst. The core differentiating factor Baker identifies is Jensen's rare ability to retain exceptional engineering talent, which he views as the primary structural advantage separating Nvidia from peers. The investment implication is a long-term, high-conviction long position in NVDA, rooted in management quality and organizational durability rather than any specific near-term catalyst or valuation argument.
detail: Baker has followed Nvidia since covering it as a small-cap semi analyst in 2000, has read every transcript, and frames Jensen alongside Hock Tan and Lisa Su as the rare semiconductor CEOs who retain exceptional engineering talent — a key differentiator.
confidence_evidence: 25-year continuous coverage, personal relationship with Jensen from early career, repeated superlatives
quote_fragment: literally following Nvidia closely for the last 25 years

ticker_audit.reasoning: This thesis is specifically and entirely about Nvidia and Jensen Huang's exceptional leadership; NVDA is the direct subject of Baker's long-term conviction.
